name: The Jagged Global Economy: National AI Exposure Derived Tables
description: Derived aggregate tables supporting a double-blind NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations & Datasets submission on national structural AI exposure. The release contains measured derived outputs only and no synthetic data.
citeAs: Anonymous. The Jagged Global Economy: National AI Exposure Derived Tables. NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations & Datasets submission artifact.

distribution.name: nation_exposure_enriched.csv
distribution.description: Country-level structural AI exposure scores with coverage, reliability, income, wage, labor-share, and white-collar variables.

distribution.name: nation_exposure_by_sex.csv
distribution.description: Country-level structural AI exposure scores by sex and total workforce where measured.

distribution.name: isco2_exposure_scores.csv
distribution.description: Occupation-level exposure scores aggregated to ISCO-08 2-digit occupations.

distribution.name: gender_gap.csv
distribution.description: Country-level male and female exposure estimates and measured gender exposure gaps.

distribution.name: gender_wc_decomposition.csv
distribution.description: Country-level decomposition used to quantify how much white-collar composition explains measured gender exposure gaps.

distribution.name: gender_wcs_decomposition.csv
distribution.description: Country-level decomposition used to quantify how much white-collar plus sales composition explains measured gender exposure gaps.

distribution.name: white_collar_analysis.csv
distribution.description: Country-level white-collar shares and white-collar / blue-collar exposure components.

distribution.name: wc_bc_sensitivity.csv
distribution.description: Sensitivity of the exposure relationship to alternative white-collar / blue-collar occupational boundaries.

distribution.name: wage_weighted_exposure.csv
distribution.description: Country-level exposure estimates with available ISCO wage weighting.

distribution.name: labor_share.csv
distribution.description: Labor-share data used to contextualize exposure through national labor dependence.

distribution.name: occupation_contributions.csv
distribution.description: Country-occupation contribution table used to decompose national exposure.

distribution.name: alternative_exposure_occupation.csv
distribution.description: ISCO-level comparison of primary Gmyrek scores with the Eloundou et al. exposure estimate.

distribution.name: alternative_exposure_national.csv
distribution.description: National exposure recomputed under the Eloundou et al. occupational exposure measure.

distribution.name: wc_boundary_sensitivity.csv
distribution.description: Robustness table for exposure results under alternative occupation-boundary definitions.

distribution.name: adoption_gap.csv
distribution.description: Country-level table comparing structural exposure with observed adoption/usage measures where available.

distribution.name: occupation_validation.csv
distribution.description: Occupation-level validation table for exposure and observed AI use patterns.

distribution.name: observed_outcomes_vs_exposure.csv
distribution.description: Paper Section 6 country-level analysis table for observed AI usage/adoption outcomes.

distribution.name: observed_outcomes_vs_exposure_stats.csv
distribution.description: Model statistics for Section 6 observed AI usage/adoption outcome fits.

distribution.name: adoption_predictor_grid.csv
distribution.description: R-squared grid for three adoption outcomes and eight predictor combinations.

distribution.name: microsoft_ai_diffusion_country_adoption.csv
distribution.description: Official Microsoft AI Diffusion country/economy adoption rates for H1 2025, H2 2025, and Q1 2026, matched to ISO alpha-3 codes.

distribution.name: appendix_predicting_adoption_regressions.csv
distribution.description: Coefficient-level OLS results for adoption-predictivity appendix models.

distribution.name: appendix_predicting_exposure_regressions.csv
distribution.description: Coefficient-level OLS results for exposure-predictivity appendix models.

distribution.name: remittance_weighted_exposure.csv
distribution.description: Country-level direct and remittance-accounted exposure estimates used in the indirect exposure section.

distribution.name: remittance_data.csv
distribution.description: Derived remittance summary variables used for indirect exposure analysis.

distribution.name: region_income_exposure_grid.csv
distribution.description: Complete 7 region by 4 income-group exposure grid using measured exposure data and no imputation.

distribution.name: region_exposure_marginals.csv
distribution.description: Regional marginal exposure aggregates with macro and labor-force-weighted estimates.

distribution.name: income_exposure_marginals.csv
distribution.description: Income-group marginal exposure aggregates with macro and labor-force-weighted estimates.

distribution.name: wb_group_exposure_exclusions.csv
distribution.description: Countries excluded from World Bank group exposure aggregates because measured exposure or classification data were unavailable.

distribution.name: national_exposure_map_data.csv
distribution.description: Country-level table used for the national exposure map.

distribution.name: sample_coverage_summary.csv
distribution.description: Paper sample coverage summary table.

distribution.name: country_exclusion_lists.csv
distribution.description: Country exclusion lists and reasons used in paper sample accounting.

distribution.name: structural_baseline_metrics.csv
distribution.description: Paper-facing structural baseline statistics.

distribution.name: cross_country_labor_composition.csv
distribution.description: Paper-facing table for labor composition and national exposure patterns.

distribution.name: validation_stack_compact.csv
distribution.description: Compact paper-facing validation summary table.

distribution.name: gmyrek_hierarchy_summary.csv
distribution.description: Paper-facing summary of the task-to-occupation aggregation hierarchy used to construct national AI exposure.

distribution.name: remittance_corridor_evidence.csv
distribution.description: Selected derived corridor evidence table for paper narrative claims, using KNOMAD 2021 bilateral remittance flows and World Bank 2024 remittance indicators where available.

distribution.name: exposure_predictor_country_panel.csv
distribution.description: Country-level predictor panel used to reproduce appendix exposure regressions, including white-collar share, log GNI, internet penetration, and O*NET cognitive-minus-physical score.

distribution.name: alternative_exposure_summary.csv
distribution.description: Aggregate robustness correlations for alternative occupational exposure measures. H&L transformed score vectors are not redistributed because no standalone open-data redistribution license was identified.

distribution.name: gender_decomposition_summary.csv
distribution.description: R-squared summary for white-collar and white-collar-plus-sales decompositions of measured gender exposure gaps.

distribution.name: openai_rank_quintile_summary.csv
distribution.description: Derived top/bottom exposure-quintile summary comparing national exposure ranks with OpenAI Signals matched-sample ranks.

recordSet.name: National AI exposure with enrichment variables
recordSet.description: Country-level structural AI exposure scores with coverage, reliability, income, wage, labor-share, and white-collar variables.

field.name: country_code
field.description: ISO-3 country or economy code. Unit/format: ISO-3 text code. Missingness: Missing only where a row is not country-specific.

field.name: country_name
field.description: Country or economy name used in the analysis. Unit/format: text. Missingness: Missing only where a row is not country-specific.

field.name: weighted_exposure
field.description: National AI exposure score computed from occupational employment shares and ISCO exposure scores. Unit/format: unitless share-like score. Missingness: No missing values in the main measured exposure panel.

field.name: n_occupations
field.description: Released analysis column `n_occupations`; see the table description for context. Unit/format: source-specific. Missingness: Blank cells denote unavailable or not-applicable values.

field.name: employment_coverage
field.description: Share of relevant employment covered by measured 2-digit ISCO occupational rows. Unit/format: proportion. Missingness: No missing values in the main measured exposure panel.

field.name: nec_share
field.description: Share of employment in not-elsewhere-classified occupational rows. Unit/format: proportion. Missingness: No missing values in the main measured exposure panel.

field.name: reliability
field.description: Project reliability category based on coverage, NEC share, occupation count, and unreliable cells. Unit/format: categorical text. Missingness: No missing values in released core panel.

field.name: gni_ppp

rai:dataLimitations: The tables measure structural exposure rather than realized wage, job-loss, or welfare effects. Coverage is limited by available ILO occupational data, 2-digit ISCO aggregation, missing major economies, vendor-specific adoption statistics, and the vintage of the KNOMAD bilateral remittance matrix.
rai:dataBiases: The source statistics have uneven coverage across countries and occupations. Observed AI adoption measures come from provider-published aggregates with different product populations, measurement periods, and definitions.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: The released tables are aggregate country- and occupation-level outputs. They do not contain individual-level records or direct personal identifiers.
rai:dataUseCases: The tables are intended for reproducing descriptive analyses of national AI exposure, adoption correlations, robustness checks, and policy-relevant cross-country comparisons. They should not be used as direct forecasts of job loss or country-level harm.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Potential positive uses include national AI and economic policy planning. Potential harms include misreading exposure rankings as job-loss rankings or using lower direct exposure to neglect countries with indirect exposure.
